rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY R.F. COLLINS VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Albay Construction Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 18, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARY BUDDLE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 18, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Tamara Vlasyuk is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell informs Ms. Vlasyuk of this tentative ruling and she states that she does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 10:00a.m., not 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD PALERMO VS. ACE HARDWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Payment Of Settlement Funds By Dap, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 18, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Plaintiffs' motion for order compelling payment of settlement funds by DAP, Inc. is continued on the court's own motion to January 6, 2015. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD KEITH ANDERS ET AL VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sears, Roebuck And Co.S Motion To Bifurcate Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 18, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 4, Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.'s motion to bifurcate plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is continued on the court's own motion to January 6, 2015. = (503/TLJ)
